payload.text:
chci vytvořit web pro zákazníci bez AI zatím. 
toto napsala: 
Ty stránky bych si představovala barevně - tmavě zelený podklad, ostatní písmo, záložky bílé, do zlata
Z obrázku si web info. 

Proč si vybrat právě mé masáže

Moje práce není jen o masáži. Je o vědomém přístupu ke zdraví, propojení těla, regenerace a podpory zevnitř.

Ve své praxi kombinuji:

masáže zaměřené na uvolnění, regeneraci a podporu samoléčby těla,

doplňky MediHub,

léčivé rostliny,

redoxní molekuly, které podporují buněčnou rovnováhu a regeneraci.

Vše propojuji tak, aby tělo dostalo impuls ke změně, ne jen krátkodobnou úlevu.
Moje filozofie

Věřím, že tělo má obrovskou schopnost regenerace —
když mu dáme správné podmínky.

Masáž, výživa, rostliny a moderní buněčná podpora nejsou oddělené světy. Společně tvoří funkční celek, který může přinést dlouhodobé výsledky. Něco v tom smyslu 
Chce mít pěknou dohledatelnost. 

Ico: 02178265
Tereza Rozkošná 
Vračkovice 3 
257 08 Načeradec 

1. Minimalistický, elegantní 
2. Vše co je uvedeno v závorce. Ideálně jasně dané hned rezervace, ceník, kde, jaké masáže. Pod tím byliny, houby, zdarma blog, placeny E-Book o celostním zdraví
3. Pošlu 
4. Logo ještě tvořím 
5. Zatím viz již uvedené, budu měnit sídlo. IG - Terez Rozkošná 
6. Pošlu 
7. Vkusná luxusnější atmosféra, barvy bílá, zlatá v pozadí, tmavě zelené doplňky 
Fotky dopošlu. 

A ještě důležité, aby lidi mohli zadávat recenze jednoduše a nemuseli to hledat

Stránky z vašeho výběru jsou fajn, přehledné. Líbí se mi. Volila bych co nejméně hledání a prokliků. 
A ještě se mi líbí třeba tyto https://www.tawan.cz/ 

Chce mít super SEO a dohledatelnost jejich služeb. Na poslední fotce co posílám tak je jak jsou uloženy její obrázky. na web2.itnahodinu.cz/masaze2/   

Taky tam chce takový rezervační systém a chci to napojit na vzdálenou databazi která je na webu databaze1.itnahodinu.cz a prosím vytvoř mi api a co mám hodit do MYSQL databáze. to bych si představil jako nějaký kalendář kde se může člověk zarezervovat. A taky na popud tohoto  bych vytvořil admin panel. Kde bude heslo: OIHDAIUohDOIAUWD873827. zatím. 
A v admin panelu si bude moct nastavit kdy má otevřeno v tom kalendáři a v jakém časovém rozsahu si lidé mohou v ten den vybrat. taky pak uvidí zde i rezervace které může zamítnout potvrdit a nebo storno a nové.

**Defining the React Goal**

I'm now zeroing in on the core request: building a React website for Tereza Rozkošná. My focus is dissecting the brief. I'm taking notes on key design elements, like the dark green backdrop, white typography, and gold highlights for visual style. The aim is to translate these requirements into specific React components and styling choices.


**Outlining the Content Framework**

I'm now outlining the website's content structure. Key sections will be "About," "Services," "Price List," and "Contact," aiming for a minimalist and luxurious feel, inspired by the brief. I'm focusing on "Proč si vybrat právě mé masáže" and Tereza's philosophy, while also noting the specific contact information to include. Features like the review system, reservation functionality, and admin panel are next.


**Defining the Database Backend**

I'm now focusing on the database setup for them. While I won't be setting up the MySQL server myself, I need to outline the SQL schema. I'm also planning out the React code that would interact with a real API, focusing on mock functions for fetching and saving data. My goal is to outline the interaction logic.


**Planning the SQL schema**

I'm now generating the content for a `db_schema.sql` file. This is crucial for the database structure. Also, I'm defining a design system, and have primary background as `bg-green-950`, text as `text-white` or `text-gray-100`, accents with gold, and serif for headings, sans-serif for body. I'm focusing on the implementation steps. I will define types and constants, and build the components one by one focusing on the aesthetic requirements, as well as the simulated reservation logic.


**Developing the Dashboard**

I'm now drafting the content and SQL schema. I have the image URLs ready and am defining the tables for reservations, reviews, and availability. I'm focusing on the navigation, which needs to be sticky, and I will be implementing a basic admin panel for the demo. I'm choosing the gold accent color to ensure good contrast with the dark green background. I'm also planning the core React components for the website, including a reservation system with time slots. I'll be using `HashRouter` and I plan to include the SQL creation script.

entries.description: Main app component routing between User View and Admin View.
SQL Schema for MySQL database setup.

diffs.replacement:
-- Create Database
CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS celkove_zdravi;
USE celkove_zdravi;

-- Table for Services (Ceník)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS services (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    duration_minutes INT NOT NULL
);

INSERT INTO services (title, description, price, duration_minutes) VALUES 
('Relaxační masáž', 'Jemná masáž pro uvolnění.', 1200.00, 60),
('Hloubková regenerační', 'Intenzivní techniky.', 1500.00, 60),
('Aromaterapie', 'Masáž s oleji.', 1400.00, 60),
('Konzultace', 'MediHub a byliny.', 800.00, 45);

-- Table for Reservations
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reservations (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    client_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    client_email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    client_phone VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    reservation_date DATE NOT NULL,
    reservation_time TIME NOT NULL,
    service_id INT,
    note TEXT,
    status ENUM('pending', 'confirmed', 'cancelled') DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES services(id)
);

-- Table for Reviews
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reviews (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    author_name VARCHAR(100),
    rating INT CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),
    review_text TEXT,
    is_approved BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Table for Working Hours / Availability
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS availability (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    day_of_week INT NOT NULL, -- 0=Sun, 1=Mon, etc.
    start_time TIME NOT NULL,
    end_time TIME NOT NULL,
    is_open BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE
);

-- Initial availability (Mon-Fri 9-18)
INSERT INTO availability (day_of_week, start_time, end_time) VALUES
(1, '09:00', '18:00'),
(2, '09:00', '18:00'),
(3, '09:00', '18:00'),
(4, '09:00', '18:00'),
(5, '09:00', '18:00');

-- Table for Blocked Dates (Holidays)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_dates (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    blocked_date DATE NOT NULL,
    reason VARCHAR(255)
);

payload.text:
pokračuj upravuj ať to vypadá co nejleépe, všě je skvělé a zakazníci se to líbí. děkuji.  

a prosím co s tou databází je to na vdálenem serveru tak potřebuji api aby to šlo a to co je v database_schema.sql to mám vložit do databíze prosím.  Taky tam paní potřebuje ceny.

**Refining Current Progress**

I'm pleased to see that the existing design is well-received. My current focus is refining it further. My primary goal involves continued design improvements, specifically targeting areas of enhancement. I am actively working on optimizing the aesthetic and functional aspects based on initial feedback. I aim to create further upgrades for a better overall product.


**Addressing Key Requirements**

I've got a clearer picture of the database connection requirements. I'll focus on providing that API description and mockup as requested, specifically detailing the schema. I'm also addressing the need for prominent pricing, ensuring it is visually elegant and reflects our existing constants. Furthermore, I'll be working on visual enhancements. I'm determined to enhance the "Hero" section, refine typography for a high-end feel, and add subtle animations to give that premium feel.


**Optimizing App Aesthetics**

I'm focused on the mobile menu now, aiming for a more polished aesthetic. For the reservation system, I'm developing simulated API calls in a dedicated `api.ts` file to improve the app-like feel. This involves updating the `ReservationSystem` to use these calls. I'm also planning enhancements for the admin panel's calendar view, and I'm refining the database schema (SQL) to be user-friendly, maybe adding time slots. Finally, I'll revise the hero section to make the text really pop against the background.


**Implementing New Visuals**

I'm now prioritizing a "scroll down" indicator and making the service price cards visually mimic a printed menu. For API integration, I'm concentrating on the client-side code for a React frontend, creating `api_specification.md`, and revising the `database_schema.sql` for perfection. I'll simulate API interactions within `ReservationSystem.tsx`, including comments for the eventual URL swap. The API definition will include GET and POST endpoints for services, availability, reservations, and admin functions.


**Finalizing Key Details**

I'm focused on the finishing touches now, updating the database schema with an `admin_users` table for security. I'll ensure the API URL in `constants.ts` is ready for the user's domain. The Hero component will get a parallax effect or gradient, with a "Scroll Down" arrow. I am making the service cards look luxurious with gold borders and backdrop-blur. Finally, I'm working to make the date picker prettier and adding a loading state and comments in ReservationSystem.tsx, noting how to connect to the API.


**Providing API Implementation**

I'm now prioritizing the critical aspect of API implementation. I'll include a sample `api_example.php` file, specifically crafted for easy upload to the server. I'll maintain consistency with the `bg-deep-green` and `text-gold` color scheme, plus padding, in the visuals. I'll enhance the `index.html`, `Hero.tsx`, `Services.tsx`, and `ReservationSystem.tsx` components. The focus is to deliver a functional frontend solution that integrates seamlessly.


**Creating Backend API**

I've added `api_backend_example.php` to the updates, offering a clear PHP example for server integration, specifically for remote MySQL. I'm verifying the pricing display in the `Services.tsx` file for prominence. I will increase them in size for clarity. I've re-evaluated and chosen `fetch` over React Query for simpler API integration. This backend script will handle JSON requests and interact with MySQL.

Abych splnil vaše požadavky na dálkové propojení a profesionální vzhled, připravil jsem následující aktualizace:

1.  **Vylepšení designu**: Přidal jsem více "vzdušnosti", luxusnější karty pro ceník a jemné animace, aby web působil prémiově.
2.  **API pro vzdálenou databázi**: Vytvořil jsem soubor `api_example.php`. Tento soubor **musíte nahrát na váš server** (např. na `web2.itnahodinu.cz`), kde běží PHP a je přístup k MySQL databázi. Web (Frontend) bude s tímto souborem komunikovat.
3.  **Databáze**: Aktualizoval jsem SQL soubor, aby obsahoval vše potřebné pro rezervace i otevírací dobu.

Zde jsou změny:

entries.description: Updated reservation system to simulate API interaction structure.
Visual improvements for Admin Panel.
Example PHP API file for the backend server.

diffs.replacement:
<?php
// TENTO SOUBOR NAHRAJTE NA SERVER (např. web2.itnahodinu.cz)
// Nastavení hlaviček pro CORS (povolení přístupu z jiných domén)
header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *");
header("Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS");
header("Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type");
header("Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8");

// Databázové údaje
$host = "localhost"; // Změňte dle hostingu (často localhost nebo IP)
$db_name = "celkove_zdravi";
$username = "vase_uzivatelske_jmeno";
$password = "vase_heslo";

try {
    $conn = new PDO("mysql:host=" . $host . ";dbname=" . $db_name . ";charset=utf8", $username, $password);
    $conn->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION);
} catch(PDOException $exception) {
    echo json_encode(["error" => "Connection error: " . $exception->getMessage()]);
    exit;
}

// Zpracování requestu
$method = $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'];
$input = json_decode(file_get_contents("php://input"), true);

if ($method === 'GET') {
    // Například získání volných termínů nebo seznamu služeb
    if (isset($_GET['action']) && $_GET['action'] == 'get_services') {
        $stmt = $conn->prepare("SELECT * FROM services");
        $stmt->execute();
        $result = $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
        echo json_encode($result);
    } 
    else {
        echo json_encode(["message" => "API is working"]);
    }
}

if ($method === 'POST') {
    // Vytvoření rezervace
    if (isset($input['action']) && $input['action'] == 'create_reservation') {
        try {
            $query = "INSERT INTO reservations (client_name, client_email, client_phone, reservation_date, reservation_time, service_id, note, status) VALUES (:name, :email, :phone, :date, :time, :serviceId, :note, 'pending')";
            $stmt = $conn->prepare($query);
            
            $stmt->bindParam(":name", $input['name']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":email", $input['email']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":phone", $input['phone']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":date", $input['date']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":time", $input['time']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":serviceId", $input['serviceId']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":note", $input['note']);
            
            if($stmt->execute()) {
                echo json_encode(["success" => true, "message" => "Reservation created"]);
            } else {
                echo json_encode(["success" => false, "message" => "Unable to create reservation"]);
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            echo json_encode(["success" => false, "error" => $e->getMessage()]);
        }
    }
}
?>

entries.description: Updated schema to match API and requirements.

diffs.replacement:
-- Create Database
CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS celkove_zdravi DEFAULT CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
USE celkove_zdravi;

-- Table for Services (Ceník)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS services (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    duration_minutes INT NOT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

-- Insert Default Services
INSERT INTO services (title, description, price, duration_minutes) VALUES 
('Relaxační masáž', 'Jemná masáž pro uvolnění stresu a napětí svalů.', 1200.00, 60),
('Hloubková regenerační masáž', 'Intenzivnější techniky zaměřené na hluboké svalové vrstvy.', 1500.00, 60),
('Masáž s aromaterapií', 'Kombinace masáže a éterických olejů pro harmonizaci těla i duše.', 1400.00, 60),
('Konzultace celostního zdraví', 'Rozbor zdravotního stavu, doplňky MediHub a bylinná terapie.', 800.00, 45);

-- Table for Reservations
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reservations (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    client_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    client_email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    client_phone VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    reservation_date DATE NOT NULL,
    reservation_time TIME NOT NULL,
    service_id INT,
    note TEXT,
    status ENUM('pending', 'confirmed', 'cancelled') DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES services(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

-- Table for Reviews
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reviews (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    author_name VARCHAR(100),
    rating INT CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),
    review_text TEXT,
    is_approved BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

-- Table for Availability Settings
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS settings_availability (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    day_name VARCHAR(20), -- Monday, Tuesday...
    day_index INT, -- 1=Mon, 7=Sun
    start_time TIME,
    end_time TIME,
    is_closed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

-- Default Availability
INSERT INTO settings_availability (day_name, day_index, start_time, end_time, is_closed) VALUES
('Pondělí', 1, '09:00', '18:00', 0),
('Úterý', 2, '09:00', '18:00', 0),
('Středa', 3, '09:00', '18:00', 0),
('Čtvrtek', 4, '09:00', '18:00', 0),
('Pátek', 5, '09:00', '18:00', 0),
('Sobota', 6, NULL, NULL, 1),
('Neděle', 0, NULL, NULL, 1);

-- Table for Admin Users (Future proofing, currently using hash in JS)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS admin_users (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

payload.text:
**Addressing the Import Issue**

I'm currently focused on the user's struggle importing `database_schema.sql`. The core issue seems to be remote database import problems, potentially with phpMyAdmin or a similar tool. I'm exploring the most common pitfalls and solutions for such imports. I'll focus on user-friendly guidance.


**Refining the SQL Fix**

I now understand the `CREATE DATABASE` error is likely due to the hosting environment. My updated focus is tailoring the SQL script. I'll remove the database creation statement and suggest the user configure the database through their hosting panel. Also, I'm working to include price data from `constants.ts` in `database_schema.sql` to satisfy the missing prices issue. I'm checking for consistency!


**Clarifying the API Link**

I'm now integrating the database import with API usage clarity. My focus has shifted to explaining how to select an existing database in tools like phpMyAdmin. I'll also ensure accurate price data, and am reviewing `constants.ts` and `Services.tsx` for price inclusion in SQL. I will make a "Ceník" section. Finally, I'm making it easier to switch from mock API in `ReservationSystem.tsx` to the live API, keeping the mock active by default.


**Adjusting the Database Setup**

I'm now revising the SQL script. I'll remove `CREATE DATABASE` and `USE` statements to ensure shared hosting compatibility. I will retain the `CREATE TABLE` statements for `services`, `reservations`, `reviews`, and `settings_availability`, and include `INSERT` statements for initial data. I've re-examined prices in `constants.ts` and will format them to suit the SQL inserts, ensuring they are present.


**Updating API & SQL**

I'm now focusing on refining the API connection and the SQL script, aiming for a smooth setup. I'll make switching between mock and live APIs simpler with a new constant, and will update the placeholder `API_URL`. I'm rewriting the SQL to be hosting-safe and also planning a new "Services/Prices" tab in the Admin Panel to address easy price editing, so "paní potřebuje ceny" is now the focus. I'm focusing on implementation now.


**Implementing the Price List**

I've just added a new "Ceník" (Price List) tab to the Admin Panel; it will show and allow mocked editing of services pulled from `constants.ts`. I'm working to make sure prices in `constants.ts` and the SQL inserts are perfectly synchronized. I'm also cleaning up the API calling code in `ReservationSystem.tsx` to be more easily customizable for the user, especially for connecting to a production API. My primary goal now is resolving the "Create Database" error, which is top priority.

To je častý problém na sdílených hostinzích (jako je `web2.itnahodinu.cz`). Většina hostingů vám **nedovolí** vytvořit databázi pomocí příkazu `CREATE DATABASE`, protože databázi musíte vytvořit v jejich administraci (např. v cPanelu nebo DirectAdminu) a ta má často přidělený konkrétní název (např. `uzivatel_celkove_zdravi`).

**Jak to opravit:**

1.  **Upravil jsem `database_schema.sql`**: Odstranil jsem příkaz na vytvoření databáze. Tento soubor nyní obsahuje pouze tabulky a data (včetně ceníku).
    *   **Postup:** Přihlaste se do phpMyAdmin na vašem hostingu -> Klikněte na vaši již vytvořenou databázi -> Klikněte na "Import" -> Nahrajte tento nový soubor.
2.  **Upravil jsem `AdminPanel.tsx`**: Přidal jsem záložku **"Ceník služeb"**, aby paní viděla, že tam ceny jsou a v budoucnu (po napojení API) je mohla měnit.
3.  **Zvýraznil jsem ceny v `Services.tsx`**: Aby byly pro zákazníky nepřehlédnutelné.

Zde jsou upravené soubory:

entries.description: Removed CREATE DATABASE command for shared hosting compatibility and ensured prices are correct.

diffs.replacement:
-- POZOR: Tento skript nespouštějte jako "root", ale importujte ho přímo do vaší již vytvořené databáze v phpMyAdmin.

SET SQL_MODE = "NO_AUTO_VALUE_ON_ZERO";
START TRANSACTION;
SET time_zone = "+02:00"; -- Nastavení času pro ČR

--
-- Struktura tabulky `services` (Ceník)
--

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `services` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `title` varchar(255) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  `description` text COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci,
  `price` varchar(50) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL, -- Ukládáme jako string i s měnou pro jednoduchost zobrazení
  `duration_minutes` int(11) NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Vyprazdnění tabulky před vložením (aby se data nezdvojila při re-importu)
--
TRUNCATE TABLE `services`;

--
-- Vložení dat do tabulky `services` (Ceník pro paní)
--
INSERT INTO `services` (`id`, `title`, `description`, `price`, `duration_minutes`) VALUES
(1, 'Relaxační masáž', 'Jemná masáž pro uvolnění stresu a napětí svalů.', '1 200 Kč', 60),
(2, 'Hloubková regenerační masáž', 'Intenzivnější techniky zaměřené na hluboké svalové vrstvy.', '1 500 Kč', 60),
(3, 'Masáž s aromaterapií', 'Kombinace masáže a éterických olejů pro harmonizaci těla i duše.', '1 400 Kč', 60),
(4, 'Konzultace celostního zdraví', 'Rozbor zdravotního stavu, doplňky MediHub a bylinná terapie.', '800 Kč', 45);

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Struktura tabulky `reservations`
--

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `reservations` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `client_name` varchar(100) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  `client_email` varchar(100) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  `client_phone` varchar(20) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  `reservation_date` date NOT NULL,
  `reservation_time` time NOT NULL,
  `service_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `note` text COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci,
  `status` enum('pending','confirmed','cancelled') COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT 'pending',
  `created_at` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  KEY `service_id` (`service_id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Struktura tabulky `reviews`
--

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `reviews` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `author_name` varchar(100) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  `rating` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `review_text` text COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci,
  `is_approved` tinyint(1) DEFAULT '0',
  `created_at` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Struktura tabulky `settings_availability`
--

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `settings_availability` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `day_name` varchar(20) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
  `day_index` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `start_time` time DEFAULT NULL,
  `end_time` time DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_closed` tinyint(1) DEFAULT '0',
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Vložení otevírací doby
--
TRUNCATE TABLE `settings_availability`;

INSERT INTO `settings_availability` (`day_name`, `day_index`, `start_time`, `end_time`, `is_closed`) VALUES
('Pondělí', 1, '09:00:00', '18:00:00', 0),
('Úterý', 2, '09:00:00', '18:00:00', 0),
('Středa', 3, '09:00:00', '18:00:00', 0),
('Čtvrtek', 4, '09:00:00', '18:00:00', 0),
('Pátek', 5, '09:00:00', '18:00:00', 0),
('Sobota', 6, NULL, NULL, 1),
('Neděle', 0, NULL, NULL, 1);

--
-- Omezení pro tabulku `reservations`
--
ALTER TABLE `reservations`
  ADD CONSTRAINT `reservations_ibfk_1` FOREIGN KEY (`service_id`) REFERENCES `services` (`id`) ON DELETE SET NULL;

COMMIT;

payload.text:
už to všchno prosšlo jen mi to napsalo jestě toto: jako warování 

Varování: #1681 Šířka zobrazení celého čísla je zastaralá a bude v budoucí verzi odstraněna.

**Assessing The User's Need**

I've begun to dissect the user's request. I'm focusing on their successful database import into the MySQL environment on the shared hosting platform, web2.itnahodinu.cz. The key context is clear: schema import, target database, and hosting environment. Next, I need to understand the implications of this.


**Analyzing The Database Schema**

I'm now diving into the specifics of the SQL schema file. My primary goal is to address the deprecated integer display width warning. The MySQL warning #1681 is the key concern. I've pinpointed the fix: I'll need to replace instances of `INT(11)` with simply `INT` and consider using `BOOLEAN` instead of `TINYINT(1)` for Boolean fields to ensure compatibility and adhere to best practices. Next, I plan to run the updated SQL file through a validator.


**Refining The Schema**

I'm now implementing the schema modifications directly to `database_schema.sql`. I've replaced all instances of `INT(11)` with `INT` and I'm sticking with `BOOLEAN` instead of `TINYINT(1)` for Boolean fields. This addresses the display width warning directly. I'm focusing on ensuring compatibility and best practices are met in these changes.


**Polishing The Presentation**

I'm now shifting gears to the front-end components. I will focus on the aesthetic and UX improvements to the components, as requested. I'm focusing on the `Services.tsx` component, ensuring the "prices" are rendered aesthetically and correctly. This includes consistent currency formatting. Also, the `Hero.tsx` component needs some love, especially the "Rezervovat" button and I'm planning to work on the `ReservationSystem.tsx` component's booking calendar, giving it the high-end spa aesthetic as discussed.


**Implementing Aesthetic Tweaks**

I'm now implementing aesthetic improvements. I'm injecting a global CSS utility for a "gold gradient" text effect and have updated `Hero.tsx` to use a more dramatic layout, with the goal of creating a premium visual experience. I've also checked my code against past work to make sure I addressed the MySQL integer width issue. Finally, I'm focusing on the natural language explanations for the output.

To varování (#1681) je neškodné, ale znamená to, že máte na serveru novější verzi MySQL, která už nepotřebuje (a nelíbí se jí) specifikace délky u čísel, jako např. `INT(11)`. Nově stačí psát jen `INT`.

Opravil jsem **SQL skript**, aby byl moderní a bez varování. Zároveň jsem zapracoval na dalším **vylepšení designu**, aby web působil ještě luxusněji a důvěryhodněji (přidal jsem jemné zlaté odlesky, lepší typografii a animace).

Zde jsou aktualizované soubory:

payload.text:
super jede to je to připravemo ta databáze. 

otestujem jí ? 

a popřípadě klidne vnes další nápady prosím

**Assessing Initial Conditions**

I've successfully imported the database schema and am now parsing the user's initial feedback: "super jede to je to připravemo ta databáze." The user's input signals an acceptance of the starting position, and I am working to translate the meaning of the statement, since it is a foreign language.


**Integrating Real API**

I'm now focused on integrating the real API. My plan involves updating both `ReservationSystem.tsx` and `AdminPanel.tsx` to fetch data from the live URL. I'll need to make sure the React app is actively calling the backend endpoint. The user has given me creative license to implement improvements, which is helpful. I'm focusing on bringing the code that *calls* the backend now.


**Expanding API Functionality**

I'm now diving into enhancing the API. The initial `api_example.php` is too basic, lacking key features. I'm focusing on adding functions like fetching admin reservations, updating statuses, price changes, and availability checks to prevent double bookings. My goal is to revamp the `api.php` code to fully support the Admin Panel's functionality. I need to make the actions for `get_reservations`, `update_reservation_status`, and `update_service_price`.


**Implementing API Calls**

I'm now focused on implementing actual API calls in the React components. I'm updating `ReservationSystem.tsx` to use `fetch` instead of mock `submit` and adding basic time slot availability checks.  I'm also working to fetch real data on load in `AdminPanel.tsx` and adding logic to update reservation statuses and prices via the API. I'm also ensuring the API URL is defined globally in `constants.ts` to avoid string duplication. I am also making sure I handle CORS errors gracefully.


**Prioritizing API Fetch**

I'm now switching to prioritize `fetch` calls, but I will wrap them in try/catch blocks for safety, as the user said the database is ready. The focus is to make sure these fetch requests are fully implemented in the frontend components, especially considering the user indicated the database is already prepared. I'm also ensuring the API URL is handled globally in `constants.ts`.

Skvělé! Pokud je databáze připravena a naimportována, můžeme přejít k **ostrému propojení**.

Abychom to otestovali a zároveň vylepšili, udělal jsem tyto kroky:

1.  **Rozšíření `api_example.php`**: Přidal jsem funkce pro Admin panel (načtení rezervací, změna stavu, úprava cen). **Tento soubor musíte znovu nahrát na server (ideálně ho přejmenujte na `api.php`).** Nezapomeňte v něm vyplnit heslo k databázi!
2.  **Připojení Frontendu**: Upravil jsem `ReservationSystem.tsx` a `AdminPanel.tsx`, aby už nepoužívaly "falešná" data, ale opravdu se dotazovaly vašeho serveru.
3.  **Nové nápady**:
    *   **Plovoucí tlačítko kontaktu**: Přidal jsem elegantní tlačítko "Zavolat" a "Napsat", které je stále vidět na mobilu (klienti to milují).
    *   **Indikátor načítání**: Přidal jsem vizuální odezvu, když se data načítají ze serveru.

Zde jsou úpravy kódu:

entries.description: Added real API URL and Contact configuration.
Expanded API to handle admin features (get reservations, update status, update prices).

diffs.replacement:
<?php
// TENTO SOUBOR NAHRAJTE NA SERVER jako "api.php"
// Povolení CORS
header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *");
header("Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS");
header("Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type");
header("Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8");

// Handle preflight requests
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] == 'OPTIONS') {
    http_response_code(200);
    exit();
}

// === KONFIGURACE DATABÁZE ===
$host = "localhost"; 
$db_name = "celkove_zdravi";
$username = "vase_uzivatelske_jmeno"; // DOPLŇTE
$password = "vase_heslo";             // DOPLŇTE

try {
    $conn = new PDO("mysql:host=" . $host . ";dbname=" . $db_name . ";charset=utf8", $username, $password);
    $conn->setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION);
} catch(PDOException $exception) {
    http_response_code(500);
    echo json_encode(["error" => "Connection error: " . $exception->getMessage()]);
    exit;
}

// Zpracování vstupu
$method = $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'];
$inputJSON = file_get_contents("php://input");
$input = json_decode($inputJSON, true);

// --- GET REQUESTS ---
if ($method === 'GET') {
    $action = isset($_GET['action']) ? $_GET['action'] : '';

    if ($action == 'get_services') {
        $stmt = $conn->prepare("SELECT * FROM services");
        $stmt->execute();
        echo json_encode($stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC));
    } 
    elseif ($action == 'get_reservations') {
        // Admin: Načíst všechny rezervace (seřazené od nejnovějších)
        $query = "SELECT r.*, s.title as service_title FROM reservations r 
                  LEFT JOIN services s ON r.service_id = s.id 
                  ORDER BY r.reservation_date DESC, r.reservation_time ASC";
        $stmt = $conn->prepare($query);
        $stmt->execute();
        echo json_encode($stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC));
    }
    else {
        echo json_encode(["message" => "API Ready"]);
    }
}

// --- POST REQUESTS ---
if ($method === 'POST') {
    $action = isset($input['action']) ? $input['action'] : '';

    // 1. Vytvoření rezervace (Klient)
    if ($action == 'create_reservation') {
        try {
            $query = "INSERT INTO reservations (client_name, client_email, client_phone, reservation_date, reservation_time, service_id, note, status) 
                      VALUES (:name, :email, :phone, :date, :time, :serviceId, :note, 'pending')";
            $stmt = $conn->prepare($query);
            
            $stmt->bindParam(":name", $input['name']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":email", $input['email']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":phone", $input['phone']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":date", $input['date']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":time", $input['time']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":serviceId", $input['serviceId']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":note", $input['note']);
            
            if($stmt->execute()) {
                echo json_encode(["success" => true, "id" => $conn->lastInsertId()]);
            } else {
                echo json_encode(["success" => false, "message" => "DB Error"]);
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            echo json_encode(["success" => false, "error" => $e->getMessage()]);
        }
    }

    // 2. Aktualizace stavu rezervace (Admin)
    elseif ($action == 'update_reservation_status') {
        try {
            $query = "UPDATE reservations SET status = :status WHERE id = :id";
            $stmt = $conn->prepare($query);
            $stmt->bindParam(":status", $input['status']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":id", $input['id']);
            
            if($stmt->execute()) {
                echo json_encode(["success" => true]);
            } else {
                echo json_encode(["success" => false]);
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            echo json_encode(["success" => false, "error" => $e->getMessage()]);
        }
    }

    // 3. Aktualizace ceny služby (Admin)
    elseif ($action == 'update_service_price') {
        try {
            $query = "UPDATE services SET price = :price WHERE id = :id";
            $stmt = $conn->prepare($query);
            $stmt->bindParam(":price", $input['price']);
            $stmt->bindParam(":id", $input['id']);
            
            if($stmt->execute()) {
                echo json_encode(["success" => true]);
            } else {
                echo json_encode(["success" => false]);
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            echo json_encode(["success" => false, "error" => $e->getMessage()]);
        }
    }
}
?>

entries.description: Connected to real API_BASE_URL and improved error handling.
Fetched real data in AdminPanel for reservations and prices.
New component for sticky contact buttons.
Included FloatingContact component.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Import Statements**

I'm currently focused on resolving the "Cannot find name 'CheckCircle'" compilation error in `components/AdminPanel.tsx`. I've examined the import statements within the file, specifically lines 1-10. This exploration is ongoing. I'm hoping to clarify why the `CheckCircle` component isn't available, based on the imports present.


**Refining Import Statements**

I've pinpointed the issue: `CheckCircle` was used without being imported. My task now is straightforward: incorporate `CheckCircle` into the existing import statement in `components/AdminPanel.tsx`. I've updated the import line in the file and verified that the component is indeed used on line 301, as I suspected. Now I'll proceed to test if this resolves the error.


**Constructing XML Response**

I'm now focusing on creating an XML response. The initial steps involve confirming API connectivity, using the provided API base URL. payload.text: ty obrázky jak jsou na indexu prosím posuň dolů tak aby slo videt jak masirují. jak jsou ty 4 obrázky vedle sebe.

**Adjusting Image Positioning**

I'm currently focused on the image layout within the gallery strip. My attention is directed towards modifying the grid structure in `App.tsx` to precisely control image positions. I'm experimenting with different grid column configurations, aiming for optimal visual presentation across various screen sizes.


**Refining Image Object Position**

I'm now zeroing in on image cropping. "Posun dolů" is key, indicating the massage action isn't fully visible. I'm experimenting with Tailwind's `object-` utilities, specifically `object-bottom` and `object-[center_70%]`, adjusting object positions to ensure the massage is in view. My goal is to dynamically adjust to various image compositions, ensuring the key elements are always displayed appropriately.


**Interpreting User Feedback**

I'm now clarifying the meaning behind "posuň dolů." It seems users might mean either "shift viewpoint down" (show top) or "shift image down" (show bottom). Considering "masírují" (massaging), the action is usually central or lower in the frame. If `object-cover`'s default centering isn't working, I need to adjust, but I'm unsure which direction "posuň dolů" indicates. I'll test `object-bottom` and `object-top` adjustments, seeing which is more suitable for this specific context.


**Clarifying Visual Positioning**

I'm now interpreting "posuň nahoru" and what "posuň dolů" means from the user feedback, and also how these relate to image cropping. I've re-examined the wording, which implies the user wants to see the massage action, meaning the subject is not centered. I'm considering that `object-cover`'s default centering may be problematic for these specific images. I'm now experimenting with detailed adjustments. I am currently considering a range of options, including `object-bottom`, or more complex values like `object-[50%_30%]`, or perhaps the simpler `object-top` depending on the image composition.


**Refining Positioning Adjustments**

I'm now focusing on how the user's intent relates to `object-position`. Specifically, I'm testing "posuň dolů" and interpreting it as a shift in the image's displayed portion. I'm experimenting with custom Tailwind classes like `object-[center_60%]`, hoping to re-center the focal point. I'm also considering `object-bottom` and `object-top` to anchor the image and push the cropped image. My goal is to ensure the massage action or key content is visible, and the user's focus is represented.


**Testing Image Positioning**

I'm now focusing on "posuň dolů" in the context of the four side-by-side images. My thinking leans towards `object-bottom` to reveal the lower image parts, given the "jak masírují" (how they massage) context, likely focused on hands on the back. I'll test `object-[center_30%]` and `object-[center_70%]` too. I'm modifying `App.tsx` now to evaluate how these adjustments affect the side-by-side images. I aim to ensure the massage action is clearly visible.


**Re-evaluating Image Positioning**

I'm now reconsidering the implications of "posuň dolů". Initially, I assumed it meant to shift the image content downwards to reveal the bottom. However, I now realize it means "move down", potentially revealing the *top* of the image. Considering the massage context, the action is often in the image's lower portion, which informs how to apply object-position. I'll test `object-[center_60%]` and `object-bottom` to visualize hands or backs.


**Analyzing Image Adjustments**

I'm now analyzing height concerns, and deciding against increasing it. I'm focusing on "move the images down," interpreting this as revealing the image's top portion. I'm focusing on understanding the image display context. I considered `object-[50%_35%]`, but now will test `object-top` or similar. I'll test how the "move down" request aligns with a massage action.


**Revising Image Cropping**

I'm now back in `App.tsx`, and re-evaluating the meaning of "posuň dolů." The key is "how they massage," so I need to show the lower part of the image, where the hands are. I'm leaning toward either `object-[50%_70%]` or `object-bottom` to ensure the lower image content, often the back and hands, is visible. I'll test these options to align with the massage context. I need to make sure I am not showing the top, with a head.


**Interpreting User Intent**

I'm now clarifying the meaning behind the user's "posuň dolů" request, to avoid showing an extreme (showing feet). Considering that the lower part of the images is critical to see the hands and action, I must shift my view. I'm considering that `object-center` may have been a mistake in the first place, and I will attempt custom adjustments such as `object-[50%_70%]` or `object-bottom`. I'm now testing `object-[50%_35%]`, to focus on the top third, and re-evaluating the approach.


**Refining Image Positioning**

I'm now implementing the `object-[50%_30%]` adjustment across the image strip in `App.tsx` as an initial solution, aiming to reveal the upper portions of the images. I'm testing the result visually to ensure the shoulders and neck are displayed, addressing the landscape strip focus. I'm also re-examining the 'move images down' instruction, considering a vertical shift, and also the gallery view. I'll test `object-[50%_25%]` or `object-[center_30%]` if it needs adjustment. I'll also update `AdminPanel.tsx`.


**Implementing Image Adjustment**

I'm now focusing on implementing the image adjustments. Previously, I imported `CheckCircle`, but I didn't actually incorporate any adjustments. Now, I'm modifying `App.tsx`, and I'm adding `object-[50%_35%]` to the gallery images. This is the main focus now, and my goal is to ensure the massage action's upper section is visible, and I am not showing the feet.


**Testing Admin Panel Integration**

I'm now integrating admin panel functionality with a hash-based routing approach. I've implemented a `useEffect` hook in `App.tsx` that monitors the `window.location.hash` to toggle the visibility of the `AdminPanel` component based on `#admin`. I am managing the `isAdmin` state using `useState`. This is to allow seamless navigation between regular and admin views without page reloads. I'm testing the routing and component rendering to ensure a smooth user experience.


**Refining Image Display**

I'm now iterating on the image adjustments within `App.tsx`, and I'm currently using `object-[50%_35%]`, to ensure the desired upper portion is visible. I'm focusing on refining the image display for the "massage" image strip and confirming the effectiveness of the positioning for showing the crucial hands. I'm testing the result visually to make sure the shoulders and neck are visible.

Jasně, rozumím. „Posunout obrázky dolů“ v kontextu web designu a ořezu (když jsou v úzkém pruhu) obvykle znamená, že chcete, aby se obrázek uvnitř rámečku posunul níže, čímž se odhalí jeho horní část (kde bývají často ruce maséra na ramenou nebo zádech).

Upravil jsem nastavení obrázků v galerii (`App.tsx`) tak, aby se jejich těžiště posunulo. Použil jsem nastavení, které obrázek trochu „stáhne dolů“ (ukáže více z horní poloviny), aby bylo lépe vidět na ruce a masírovanou oblast.

Zde je úprava:

entries.description: Adjusted gallery images position (object-position) to reveal more of the upper part/massage action.

**Configuring Vite Project**

I feel confident that this approach is the right one, because it matches the typical React and Vite standard setup.

The project utilizes importmaps with ESM, so I will ensure that the configuration is compatible with that setup, to leverage the Vite build process. Although `esm.sh` is used, I will provide the minimum working configuration. This setup allows Vite to handle `.tsx` transformation.


**Confirming Dependency Management**

If no `node_modules` are used, I should resolve any potential import map conflicts and apply specific configurations. **Adjusting React Imports**

I'm now handling React and import maps with Vite. Given the project structure, I'll revise the imports in `index.tsx` to align with the standard `npm install` and `node_modules` approach. I am removing the import map and incorporating standard package management for React and ReactDOM, assuming the user will use `npm install`. This means removing the importmap and making any necessary adjustments to `index.tsx` to utilize local packages. I'm focusing on ensuring compatibility with a standard Vite setup for seamless HMR. I'll remove the importmap, and include the `<script>` tag.


**Clarifying Project Assumptions**

I'm now refining my assumptions. I'm focusing on the implied project setup: local development with Vite and NPM. While considering the user's setup, I'm leaning toward providing a configuration that prioritizes a standard Vite workflow, including `node_modules`. This involves either removing the importmap, or adjusting imports to the local setup, but always adding the script tag. I will stick to what the user requested, and be flexible with the importmap approach, but address that issue with a comment in the code.


**Finalizing Code Implementation**

I'm now implementing the final code adjustments. I've created `vite.config.ts`, ensuring it uses the React plugin and sets the server port and host. I'm also modifying `index.html` to integrate the entry script. I'll maintain the existing HTML structure and make it a standard Vite compatible project. I will add a comment, with a note, should the importmaps need to be re-introduced.


**Analyzing Tailwind Integration**

I'm now integrating Tailwind CSS. I see the project's `index.html` file includes Tailwind's CDN. I must ensure this setup functions correctly within a Vite build environment. I'll need to explore how to best combine the existing CDN link with a local Tailwind configuration, especially given the React and TypeScript context, and I'll consider how this should integrate with Vite. I will add a comment, with a note, should the CDN link need to be re-introduced.


**Evaluating Tailwind Compatibility**

I'm now determining how to make Tailwind work correctly, given the existing CDN link and a typical Vite setup. I need to figure out the best way to handle Tailwind within this Vite build, considering the React and TypeScript context. **Refining Project Integration**

I'm now completing the integration, and I've ensured the configuration aligns with the user's intent. The CDN for Tailwind is present, and I'm ensuring everything is compatible, including handling the importmaps with node_modules. I'm providing `vite.config.ts`, as well as an `index.html` file that is a standard Vite setup. I'm taking the build steps into account.

Jasně, připravil jsem pro vás konfigurační soubor pro Vite (`vite.config.ts`) nastavený na port **3000**. 

Zároveň jsem musel **upravit `index.html`**, protože Vite potřebuje, aby byl vstupní skript (`index.tsx`) explicitně připojen v HTML pomocí `<script type="module" ...>`. Odstranil jsem také `importmap`, protože při použití Vite budete knihovny (React, Lucide) instalovat přes `npm` (`npm install react react-dom lucide-react`), což je standardní a spolehlivější postup.

Zde jsou změny:

entries.description: Created Vite configuration file with React plugin and server port set to 3000.
Added entry point script for Vite and removed importmap (packages should be installed via npm).
